entry.title: Admissions System Maintenance (ADM) Maintenance June 9, 2026, 15:00-18:00 PT
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT Jun 9, 15:00 - 18:00 PDT

May 19, 11:57 PDT
Scheduled - ADM applications, including Admissions Administrative Portal (AAP), Applicant Service Centre (ASC), Broad-Based Admission (BBA), and Application Server (AS), will be unavailable on June 9, 2026, from 15:00 to 18:00 PT due to a planned server migration.
During this time, staff may not be able to access ADM applications, and students may not be able to log in to the Applicant Service Centre to accept offers, view application statuses, or complete related activities. Students, faculty, and staff are advised to plan accordingly.

entry.title: Perceptive Content and Document Viewer Maintenance June 8, 2026, 08:30–13:00 PT
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT Jun 8, 08:30 - 13:00 PDT

May 19, 11:55 PDT
Scheduled - Perceptive Content or ImageNow, and Document Viewer will be unavailable on June 8, 2026, from 08:30 to 13:00 PT due to a planned server migration. During this time, users will not be able to access Perceptive Content or view documents, including transcripts, through the Document Viewer application in Workday. Admissions staff and other users are advised to plan accordingly.

entry.title: Perceptive Content Maintenance Outage: May 28, 2026 (17:00–17:45 PT)
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT May 28, 17:00 - 17:45 PDT

May 19, 14:43 PDT
Scheduled - A scheduled maintenance activity will be performed on the Perceptive Content environment.

The RHEL server will be patched as scheduled, and all Perceptive Content application services will be unavailable during the maintenance window.

entry.title: UBCIT eSAM Servers Maintenance May 27, 2026, 20:00-23:00 PT
entry.content:
THIS IS A SCHEDULED EVENT May 27, 20:00 - 23:00 PDT

May 5, 12:12 PDT
Scheduled - UBCIT will be performing maintenance on the eSAM component and vault servers on May 27, 2026, 20:00-23:00 PT. This change will affect both UBC campuses.

Users will intermittently not be able to login to any servers managed by eSAM, nor retrieve any credentials via the eSAM Web UI throughout the maintenance window.

Any connections terminated while a component is being rebooted can be reconnected to the secondary node. All connections will be terminated and users won't be able to reconnect while we patch the vault servers (which typically lasts around 30 minutes) during this window.

entry.title: Workday Scheduled Weekly Maintenance May 22, 2026, 21:00 PT to May 23, 2026, 00:00 PT
entry.content:
May 23, 00:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 22, 21:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 19, 11:52 PDT
Scheduled - Please be advised that on May 22 2026, 21:00 PT to May 23 2026, 00:00 PT, Workday will be undergoing scheduled maintenance as part of its weekly service update. The system will remain accessible during the scheduled maintenance period but it is advised not to run critical processes and tasks.
Learn about Workday Support at UBC and other available resources on isc.ubc.ca.

entry.title: TouchNet TEST site (uCommerce 9.5) Scheduled Maintenance May 22, 2026 05:00 to 17:00 PT
entry.content:
May 22, 17:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 22, 05:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

Apr 20, 10:17 PDT
Scheduled - The TouchNet TEST site will be undergoing scheduled maintenance. During the update window the TouchNet TEST site will be offline for logging in by staff as a TouchNet user and for making TEST payments.

For any questions please contact dpp.support@ubc.ca

entry.title: UBC Enterprise Mailing Lists Service Migration May 22, 2026 10:00-12:00 PT
entry.content:
May 22, 12:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 22, 10:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 19, 14:35 PDT
Scheduled - The UBC Enterprise Mailing Lists (LISTSERV) server hosted on EduCloud will be undergoing maintenance on May 22, 2026 from 10:00-12:00 PT. The UBC Mailing Lists web portal at https://lists.ubc.ca will be unavailable during this time. Also, messages sent to @lists.ubc.ca will be delayed for delivery to recipients as they will be queued until the maintenance has completed.

entry.title: Totem Park and Place Vanier Network Switch Replacement May 21, 2026 22:00 - May 22, 2026 04:00 PT
entry.content:
May 22, 04:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 21, 22:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 20, 15:46 PDT
Scheduled - Network maintenance is being performed at the Totem Park and Place Vanier Student Residences, involving replacement of aging network infrastructure. During this service window from May 21, 2026 22:00 - May 22, 2026 04:00 PT; network services will be interrupted briefly and intermittently while physical and logical changes are being performed on the network backbones and while the user ports are migrated to new switching infrastructure.

entry.title: Learner Financial Support Management (LFSM) Maintenance May 21, 2026, 14:00-18:00 PT
entry.content:
May 21, 18:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 21, 14:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 13, 10:53 PDT
Scheduled - Learner Financial Support Management (LFSM) applications, including the UBC Bursary (Financial Profile) application and Award Offer Acceptance screen, will be unavailable from 14:00 to 18:00 PT on May 21,2026 due to a planned system maintenance update. Students, Faculty and Staff are advised to plan accordingly.

entry.title: Enterprise Automation & Solution Delivery service maintenance for Graduation Management Application 21 May 2026 12:00 - 16:00 PT
entry.content:
May 21, 16:01 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 21, 12:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 20, 15:49 PDT
Scheduled - The Enterprise Automation and Solution Delivery (EASD) Graduation Management Application will undergo maintenance from 21 May 2026 from 12:00 and validation until 21 May 2026 16:00

Apps/Services:

Bug fixes for Graduation Management Application (CNVM-Appian)
To fix the bug that Ceremony Details page is not accessible
Impacts:

During this maintenance there may be a brief interruption (from 21 May 2026 from 12:00 - 12:30) to the apps and services listed above.
Impacted Services:

ubc-iec--appian-web-graduation-management-application--1

entry.title: U-Pass BC Maintenance May 21, 2026 09:00-16:00 PT
entry.content:
May 21, 16:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 21, 09:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 19, 14:29 PDT
Scheduled - U-Pass BC Website Maintenance on May 21, 2026
On May 21, 2026 from 09:00 – 1600 PT, login to the U-Pass BC website (upassbc.translink.ca) will be unavailable due to website maintenance. During this time, the website will be online but users will not be able to login and activate their U-pass.


U-Pass BC Website Maintenance on May 21, 2026
On May 21, 2026 from 09:00 – 1600 PT, login to the U-Pass BC Admin website (https://upassbcadmin-b.translink.ca) will be unavailable due to website maintenance. During this time the website will be online, but users will not be able to login to check student account status, eligibility or card status.

entry.title: Disruption of Tableau services at UBC May 20, 2026 11.15 PT
entry.content:
May 21, 11:06 PDT
Resolved - Access to Tableau has been restored for UBC users. Users should now be able to access Tableau resources as usual.

May 20, 16:59 PDT
Update - • ETA Update: We are now targeting a resolution by 2:00 PM May 21, 2026

May 20, 13:59 PDT
Update - • ETA Update: We are now targeting a resolution by 4:00 PM today.

May 20, 11:15 PDT
Investigating - Disruption of Tableau services at UBC:
• Issue: Tableau services are currently experiencing a UBC-wide outage due to an ongoing contract renewal process.
• Impact: Users are unable to access data stored in Tableau reports, which may affect regular operations and supporting processes.
• Progress: UBC Procurement is working directly with Tableau (Salesforce) to finalize the renewal and restore full access as quickly as possible.
• ETA: We are targeting a resolution by 2:00 PM today.

entry.title: Online Advising Management System (OAMS) Scheduled Maintenance May 20, 2026, 17:30-22:30 PT
entry.content:
May 20, 22:30 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 20, 17:30 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 12, 14:15 PDT
Scheduled - The Online Advising Management System (OAMS) will undergo scheduled maintenance to facilitate a database upgrade on May 20, 2026, from 17:30 PT to 22:30 PT.
During this time, the service may be unavailable.

Please contact SIS.Support@ubc.ca for any questions or concerns.

entry.title: Canvas Unavailable May 07, 2026 - 13:22 PT
entry.content:
May 19, 17:42 PDT
Resolved - Canvas now operational - Resolved

You can now log in to Canvas and Canvas Catalog. UBC has worked with Instructure to restore access to Canvas and Canvas Catalog for the UBC community.

Please note while access has been restored to Canvas, some functionality within Canvas will take longer to fully recover.

Students should continue to check their email regularly as instructors or departments may contact them directly with updates and instructions.

Faculty and instructors: More information is available on the LT Hub page for instructors.

Students: More information is available on the LT Hub page for students.

We recognize the disruption this incident has caused for teaching, learning, and university operations.

Instructure has advised that all data affected by the incident was returned and Instructure received digital confirmation of data destruction by the unauthorized actor. More information is provided by Instructure here: https://www.instructure.com/incident_update

We recommend that students, faculty, and staff continue to be vigilant against phishing and follow best practices for protecting their accounts and data, including using passphrases or strong passwords and enabling multi-factor authentication where available. Community members can change their CWL password on the CWL myAccount Webpage.

May 15, 18:05 PDT
Update - Canvas now operational - Resolved

You can now log in to Canvas and Canvas Catalog. UBC has worked with Instructure to restore access to Canvas and Canvas Catalog for the UBC community.

Please note while access has been restored to Canvas, some functionality within Canvas will take longer to fully recover.

Students should continue to check their email regularly as instructors or departments may contact them directly with updates and instructions.

Faculty and instructors: More information is available on the LT Hub page for instructors.

Students: More information is available on the LT Hub page for students.

We recognize the disruption this incident has caused for teaching, learning, and university operations.

Instructure has advised that all data affected by the incident was returned and Instructure received digital confirmation of data destruction by the unauthorized actor. More information is provided by Instructure here: https://www.instructure.com/incident_update

We recommend that students, faculty, and staff continue to be vigilant against phishing and follow best practices for protecting their accounts and data, including using passphrases or strong passwords and enabling multi-factor authentication where available. Community members can change their CWL password on the CWL myAccount Webpage.

May 15, 15:45 PDT
Update - .

May 14, 21:51 PDT
Monitoring - This morning, UBC received information from Instructure related to the mitigations and remediations Instructure has taken to address the Canvas cybersecurity incident. Based on this new information, UBC has made the decision to work with Instructure to restore access to Canvas and Canvas Catalog for the UBC community.

Faculty who are currently teaching will be contacted with details about when Canvas access is expected to be restored.

We recognize the disruption this incident has caused for teaching, learning, and university operations, and we appreciate our community’s continued patience.

We recommend that faculty, staff, and students continue to be vigilant against phishing and follow best practices for protecting their accounts and data, including using passphrases or strong passwords and enabling multi-factor authentication where available.

May 12, 10:14 PDT
Update - .

May 11, 14:36 PDT
Update - While Instructure has made the Canvas service available again, UBC continues to review prior to providing access for the UBC community.

The university is working to maintain learning continuity for courses already underway as well as those starting later this week and will be contacting affected community members in the coming days regarding these courses. Students and instructors can find course information and resources, while Canvas is unavailable: https://ltic.ubc.ca/canvas-redirect/.

Students, please check your email from UBC regularly, as your instructor or department may contact you directly with updates or instructions.

We also recommend that faculty, staff, and students continue to be vigilant against phishing and follow best practices for protecting their accounts and data, including using strong passwords and enabling multi-factor authentication where available.

May 8, 13:05 PDT
Update - The university is working to maintain learning continuity for courses starting next week and will be contacting affected community members in the coming days.

As a precautionary measure, we are now advising UBC community members who use Canvas to take the following steps:

- Do not attempt to log into Canvas or Canvas Catalog until UBC IT advises that it is safe to do so; and
- If you logged into Canvas or Canvas Catalog after 12 p.m. PT on Thursday, May 7, please change your CWL password immediately and contact UBC Cybersecurity at security@ubc.ca. Community members can change their CWL password here: https://www.myaccount.ubc.ca/myAccount/.

While Instructure has made the Canvas service available, UBC needs to review security measures prior to providing access for the UBC community.

We also recommend that faculty, staff, and students continue to be vigilant against phishing and follow best practices for protecting their accounts and data, including using strong passwords and enabling multi-factor authentication where available.

May 7, 19:08 PDT
Update - The university is working to maintain learning continuity for courses starting next week and will be contacting affected community members in the coming days.

We also recommend that faculty, staff, and students continue to be vigilant against phishing and follow best practices for protecting their accounts and data, including using strong passwords and enabling multi-factor authentication where available.

We will provide more updates as soon as we have them.

May 7, 19:08 PDT
Update - .

May 7, 13:57 PDT
Investigating - Canvas is unavailable due to a cyber breach of its parent company Instructure. UBC community members should not log into Canvas until further notice.

If you are logged in, please log out immediately and do not log back in until you are notified by UBC IT that is safe to do so. If you have logged in after 12 p.m. PT on Thursday, May 7, please change your CWL password immediately and reach out to security@ubc.ca.

We will provide more updates as soon as we have them.

entry.title: LTIC Service Maintenance May 16, 2026 22:00-23:59:00 PT
entry.content:
May 19, 09:20 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 19, 09:15 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 19, 09:08 PDT
Scheduled - Multiple LTIC services may experience brief, intermittent interruptions due to scheduled maintenance on May 16, 2026 between 22:00–23:59 PT.

Services affected:
ComPAIR
Hotcrp
iPeer
Moodle
WeBWorK
UBC Wiki

entry.title: UBC Vancouver Campus Network Maintenance May 16, 2026, 07:00-15:00 PT
entry.content:
May 16, 15:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 16, 07:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

Apr 21, 14:27 PDT
Scheduled - The UBC Vancouver campus network will undergo maintenance on May 16, 2026, 07:00-15:00 PT. During the maintenance window, intermittent disruptions to network connectivity may be experienced, including internet, cloud services and remote VPN access.

entry.title: Workday Scheduled Quarterly Maintenance and Weekly Service Update May 15, 2026, 21:00 PT to May 16, 2026, 08:00 PT
entry.content:
May 16, 08:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 15, 21:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 14, 15:52 PDT
Update - Update: Add/drop deadline extended to Monday, May 18 at 11:59 pm (PDT) due to system maintenance

As students will be unable to login to Workday during this maintenance window, the deadline for students to add/drop course sections with a May 15 add/drop deadline has been extended until Monday, May 18 at 11:59 pm. Once the system is restored on Saturday, May 16, students will have access to the add/drop functionality for these course sections until the updated deadline above.

Students can confirm course-specific add/drop deadlines in Workday by using the Find Course Sections report, which shows the adjusted deadline for any impacted courses. Student can refer to Viewing the Course Schedule (Okanagan | Vancouver) to learn how to find an add/drop date in the Find Course Sections report.

May 11, 10:53 PDT
Scheduled - Please be advised that on May 15, 2026, 21:00 PT to May 16, 2026, 08:00 PT, Workday will be undergoing scheduled maintenance as part of its quarterly service update. During the scheduled maintenance period, Workday will be unavailable for use.
Learn about Workday Support at UBC and other available resources on isc.ubc.ca.

entry.title: Okanagan Campus Network Maintenance May 15, 2026, 19:00-22:00 PT
entry.content:
May 15, 22:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 15, 19:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 12, 14:23 PDT
Scheduled - Network maintenance will occur for the Okanagan campus on May 15, 2026, 19:00-22:00 PT. No disruption to network services is expected.

entry.title: UBC SharePoint Maintenance Friday, May 15, 2026 09:00 PT to 17:00 PT
entry.content:
May 15, 17:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 15, 09:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 11, 11:09 PDT
Scheduled - On Friday, May 15, 2026 from 09:00 PT to 17:00 PT, the UBC SharePoint Software as a Service (SaaS) service will be undergoing maintenance. During this maintenance window, SharePoint sites will be accessible as no interruptions to the service are expected.

entry.title: Library Web Catalogue (Voyager) is intermittently unavailable May 15, 2026 11.00 PT
entry.content:
May 15, 15:06 PDT
Resolved - This incident has been resolved.

May 15, 13:05 PDT
Monitoring - The service is stable and we are continuing to monitor.

May 15, 11:03 PDT
Investigating - The Library Web Catalogue (Voyager) is intermittently unavailable. Accessing the catalogue may occasionally result in an error page. If this occurs, please refresh your web browser.

UBC IT is currently working to restore normal service.

entry.title: TouchNet PROD Site Maintenance May 15, 2026 00:00 to 04:00 PT
entry.content:
May 15, 04:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 15, 00:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

Apr 20, 10:14 PDT
Scheduled - The TouchNet PROD site will be undergoing scheduled maintenance. During the update window the TouchNet PROD site will be offline for logging in by staff as a TouchNet user and for customers making new payments. During this time when the payor clicks "pay now" TouchNet will display a screen informing the payor that the system is off line for maintenance and to try again after the system is back online.

For any questions please contact dpp.support@ubc.ca

entry.title: EduCloud Server Maintenance (UBC Okanagan) May 14, 2026, 15:00-18:00 PT
entry.content:
May 14, 18:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 14, 15:00 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 12, 15:45 PDT
Scheduled - The EduCloud Server environment (https://bcnet.educloud.ubc.ca) for Organizations hosted at UBC Okanagan in Kelowna will be undergoing scheduled maintenance on May 14, 2026, 15:00-18:00 PT.

There are no expected outages to running virtual machines and no impacts to the EduCloud Server Administration Portal.

entry.title: Enterprise Automation and Solution Delivery services maintenance for Scheduling Activity Transfer from May 14, 2026 15:00 -17:00
entry.content:
May 14, 17:00 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 14, 15:01 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 13, 15:41 PDT
Scheduled - Enterprise Automation and Solution Delivery services maintenance for Scheduling Activity Transfer from May 14, 2026 15:00 -17:00 

The EASD Team will be releasing software updates that may affect Scheduling services

During this maintenance window there may be a potential interruption to the apps and services for <30 min (between 3pm to 5pm)
Release activities will be completed between from May 14, 2026  15:00 - 16:00, Validation will continue until 17:00.

Apps/Services:

Bug fixes / Enhancement for Scheduling Activity Transfer

Fix stalled activity transfer in the production environment, due to CRSS not being able to handle Scientia activities that have a blank 'Name' field.

Impacts:

No other impacts to end users.

Reason for maintenance:

IRP Student: Scheduling Activity Transfer bug fixes

entry.title: Canvas May 14, 2026 14:30 to 16:30 PT
entry.content:
May 14, 16:30 PDT
Completed - The scheduled maintenance has been completed.

May 14, 15:50 PDT
In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.

May 14, 15:46 PDT
Scheduled - UBC Canvas would undergo maintenance on May 14, 2026 14:30 to 16:30 PT. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) would be enforced for students logging into Canvas.
